# Agent Execution Unresolved Design: RAG Guardrails

<!-- Generated by go run ./tools/agentexecutiondesign -write-doc. Do not edit by hand. -->

[Back to agent-execution-unresolved-design.md](../agent-execution-unresolved-design.md)

Public-safe RAG may contain:

- vocabulary
- ownership
- non-secret rules
- generated contract shape
- test criteria

RAG index must not contain:

- private repository clone URL with credential
- raw token, bearer token, PAT, GitHub App installation token
- AWS account-specific live evidence payload
- signed URL, temporary credential, SSM parameter value
- production smoke response body containing customer/task private content

## Open Decisions

| ID | Decision needed | Default until decided |
| --- | --- | --- |
| Q-EXEC-001 | private repo auth broker shape | public repo only, private fail-closed |
| Q-EXEC-002 | long-lived provider process default | one-shot default, resume id requires explicit resume |
| Q-EXEC-003 | web approval timeout and fallback terminal state | blocked with approval_timeout |
| Q-EXEC-004 | WorkspacePlan inline vs linked execution plan id | assignment snapshot inline minimal plan |
| Q-EXEC-005 | stream delta retention window | final answer durable, deltas bounded/ephemeral |

## Non-goals

- This design does not implement private repo auth immediately.
- The public daemon does not bundle or install provider CLI binaries.
- The daemon does not own client read-model UI copy.
- The workspace materializer does not directly mutate the user's original repo working tree.
- Public repositories do not store live deployment payloads or secret evidence.
